name: Krateo PlatformOps IDP API
what_it_does: Open-source Kubernetes-native Internal Developer Portal with REST API — enabling platform engineering teams to build self-service developer portals using Crossplane-based infrastructure templates, manage GitOps workflows, and provide unified service catalog and self-service provisioning, all deployed as Kubernetes custom resources.
use_cases: Building self-service infrastructure portals via Krateo API for developer environment provisioning
Managing Krateo compositions via API for Crossplane-based infrastructure template automation
Integrating GitOps workflows via Krateo API for ArgoCD/Flux-based deployment management
Cataloging services via Krateo API for internal service discovery and team ownership
Extending Krateo via custom resource definitions for organization-specific IDP requirements
not_for: Teams without Kubernetes (Krateo requires Kubernetes as the control plane)
Managed SaaS IDP without self-hosting (Krateo is self-hosted only)
Simple service catalog without infrastructure automation
best_when: A platform engineering team wants a fully open-source, Kubernetes-native IDP that leverages Crossplane for infrastructure provisioning and GitOps for deployment, without vendor lock-in.
avoid_when: You need a managed SaaS IDP, lack Kubernetes expertise, or want a non-Crossplane infrastructure abstraction.

auth.notes: Krateo BFF (Backend for Frontend) REST API. OAuth2 via external IdP (Keycloak, Azure AD, GitHub OAuth). JWT Bearer tokens for API access. Kubernetes RBAC underneath: Krateo maps IDP permissions to Kubernetes RBAC. API at {krateo-host}/api/. Crossplane: used for infrastructure provisioning (separate Kubernetes API). ArgoCD/FluxCD: GitOps integration for deployments. kubectl: direct Kubernetes access for platform operators.

pricing.paid_tiers: Community support only; commercial support: contact Krateo
pricing.notes: Italian open-source project. Apache 2.0 license. Built on Crossplane (CNCF incubating). Components: Krateo BFF (API), Krateo Composable Portal (UI), Krateo FireWorks App (templates), Krateo Dashboard. Architecture: all components are Kubernetes operators. Crossplane providers: AWS, Azure, GCP, Helm, Kubernetes. Templates: Crossplane compositions for self-service infrastructure. GitOps: ArgoCD or Flux integration for deployment management. CNCF ecosystem: Crossplane + Argo + Krateo = full GitOps+IDP stack. Community: early-stage, growing ecosystem. Commercial support available from Krateo creators.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Kubernetes-native (not traditional API). Crossplane dependency. OAuth2 via external IdP required. Italian open-source. Early-stage. Apache 2.0. No managed SaaS. RBAC maps to Kubernetes RBAC. Community-supported.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Kubernetes-native: all resources are declarative CRDs — idempotent by design. REST API reads idempotent. Crossplane compositions: declarative and idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Kubernetes-native: all Krateo resources are Kubernetes CRDs — kubectl is a primary interaction tool
Crossplane required: Krateo uses Crossplane for infrastructure provisioning — install Crossplane first
External IdP required: Krateo needs Keycloak, Azure AD, or GitHub OAuth for user authentication
Early-stage open-source: documentation and API stability less mature than commercial IDPs
RBAC: Krateo maps portal permissions to Kubernetes RBAC — configure K8s RBAC alongside Krateo
Self-hosted only: no managed SaaS option — production deployment requires K8s operational expertise

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (via Kubernetes Ingress). OAuth2 with external IdP — strong identity management. Kubernetes RBAC for fine-grained access. Apache 2.0: auditable codebase. Self-hosted: operator responsible for all security hardening. Crossplane: manages cloud credentials (high blast radius — use proper IRSA/Workload Identity). Early-stage: fewer security audits than commercial alternatives. No SaaS — data never leaves your cluster.
